heading: National dam safety program
label: 467f National dam safety program
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (e)(1), was in the original “section 13” and was translated as meaning prior to its redesignation as section 14 by .Section 467j of this titlesection 13 of Pub. L. 92–367Pub. L. 113–121, § 3001(d)(1)

children.text: be administered by FEMA to achieve the objectives set forth in subsection (c);
involve, to the extent appropriate, each Federal agency; and

children.label: A
children.text: each of the components described in subsection (d);

children.label: B
children.text: the strategic plan described in subsection (b); and

children.label: C
children.text: assistance for State dam safety programs described in subsection (e).
to establish goals, priorities, performance measures, and target dates toward effectively administering this subchapter in order to improve the safety of dams in the United States; and
to the extent feasible, to establish cooperation and coordination with, and assistance to, interested governmental entities in all States.
ensure that new and existing dams are safe through the development of technologically and economically feasible programs and procedures for national dam safety hazard reduction;
encourage acceptable engineering policies and procedures to be used for dam site investigation, design, construction, operation and maintenance, and emergency preparedness;
encourage the establishment and implementation of effective dam safety programs in each State based on State standards;
develop and implement a comprehensive dam safety hazard education and public awareness initiative to assist the public in preparing for, mitigating, responding to, and recovering from dam incidents;
develop technical assistance materials for Federal and non-Federal dam safety programs;
develop mechanisms with which to provide Federal technical assistance for dam safety to the non-Federal sector; and
develop technical assistance materials, seminars, and guidelines to improve security for dams in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: a Federal element and a non-Federal element; and

children.label: B
children.text: leadership activity, technical assistance activity, and public awareness activity.

children.heading: Federal
children.label: A Federal
children.text: The Federal element shall incorporate the activities and practices carried out by Federal agencies under to implement the Federal Guidelines for Dam Safety.section 467e of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the activities and practices carried out by States, local governments, and the private sector to safely build, regulate, operate, and maintain dams; and

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal activities that foster State efforts to develop and implement effective programs for the safety of dams.

children.heading: Leadership
children.label: A Leadership
children.text: The leadership activity shall be the responsibility of FEMA and shall be exercised by chairing the Board to coordinate national efforts to improve the safety of the dams in the United States.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: B Technical assistance
children.text: The technical assistance activity shall consist of the transfer of knowledge and technical information among the Federal and non-Federal elements described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Public awareness
children.label: C Public awareness
children.text: The public awareness activity shall provide for the education of the public, including State and local officials, in the hazards of dam failure, methods of reducing the adverse consequences of dam failure, and related matters.

children.heading: Assistance for State dam safety programs
children.label: e Assistance for State dam safety programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To encourage the establishment and maintenance of effective State programs intended to ensure dam safety, to protect human life and property, and to improve State dam safety programs, the Administrator shall provide assistance with amounts made available under section 467j of this title to assist States in establishing, maintaining, and improving dam safety programs in accordance with the criteria specified in paragraph (2).1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: the authority to review and approve plans and specifications to construct, enlarge, modify, remove, and abandon dams;

children.label: ii
children.text: the authority to perform periodic inspections during dam construction to ensure compliance with approved plans and specifications;

children.label: iii
children.text: a requirement that, on completion of dam construction, State approval must be given before operation of the dam;

children.label: iv
children.text: the authority to require or perform periodic evaluations of all dams and reservoirs to determine the extent of the threat to human life and property in case of failure;

children.label: I
children.text: the authority to require or perform the inspection, at least once every 5 years, of all dams and reservoirs that would pose a significant threat to human life and property in case of failure to determine the continued safety of the dams and reservoirs; and

children.label: II
children.text: a procedure for more detailed and frequent safety inspections;

children.label: vi
children.text: a requirement that all inspections be performed under the supervision of a State-registered professional engineer with related experience in dam design and construction;

children.label: vii
children.text: the authority to issue notices, when appropriate, to require owners of dams to perform necessary maintenance or remedial work, install and monitor instrumentation, improve security, revise operating procedures, or take other actions, including breaching dams when necessary;

children.label: viii
children.text: regulations for carrying out the legislation of the State described in this subparagraph;

children.label: I
children.text: to ensure timely repairs or other changes to, or removal of, a dam in order to protect human life and property; and

children.label: II
children.text: if the owner of the dam does not take action described in subclause (I), to take appropriate action as expeditiously as practicable;

children.label: x
children.text: a system of emergency procedures to be used if a dam fails or if the failure of a dam is imminent; and

children.label: I
children.text: each dam the failure of which could be reasonably expected to endanger human life;

children.label: II
children.text: the maximum area that could be flooded if the dam failed; and

children.label: III
children.text: necessary public facilities that would be affected by the flooding.

children.heading: Budgeting requirement
children.label: B Budgeting requirement
children.text: For a State to be eligible for assistance under this subsection, State appropriations must be budgeted to carry out the legislation of the State under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Work plans
children.label: 3 Work plans
children.text: The Administrator shall enter into a agreement with each State receiving assistance under paragraph (2) to develop a work plan necessary for the State dam safety program to reach a level of program performance specified in the agreement.2So in original. Probably should be “an”.2

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: 4 Maintenance of effort
children.text: Assistance may not be provided to a State under this subsection for a fiscal year unless the State enters into such agreement with the Administrator as the Administrator requires to ensure that the State will maintain the aggregate expenditures of the State from all other sources for programs to ensure dam safety for the protection of human life and property at or above a level equal to the average annual level of such expenditures for the 2 fiscal years preceding the fiscal year.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: A Submission
children.text: For a State to be eligible for assistance under this subsection, a plan for a State dam safety program shall be submitted to the Administrator for approval.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: B Approval
children.text: A State dam safety program shall be deemed to be approved 120 days after the date of receipt by the Administrator unless the Administrator determines within the 120-day period that the State dam safety program fails to meet the requirements of paragraphs (1) through (3).

children.heading: Notification of disapproval
children.label: C Notification of disapproval
children.text: If the Administrator determines that a State dam safety program does not meet the requirements for approval, the Administrator shall immediately notify the State in writing and provide the reasons for the determination and the changes that are necessary for the plan to be approved.

children.heading: Review of State dam safety programs
children.label: 6 Review of State dam safety programs
children.text: Using the expertise of the Board, the Administrator shall periodically review State dam safety programs. If the Board finds that a State dam safety program has proven inadequate to reasonably protect human life and property and the Administrator concurs, the Administrator shall revoke approval of the State dam safety program, and withhold assistance under this subsection, until the State dam safety program again meets the requirements for approval.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Administrator shall establish an advisory board to be known as the “National Dam Safety Review Board” to monitor the safety of dams in the United States, to monitor State implementation of this section, and to advise the Administrator on national dam safety policy.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 2 Authority
children.text: The Board may use the expertise of Federal agencies and enter into contracts for necessary studies to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 member shall represent the Department of Agriculture;

children.label: B
children.text: 1 member shall represent the Department of Defense;

children.label: C
children.text: 1 member shall represent the Department of the Interior;

children.label: D
children.text: 1 member shall represent FEMA;

children.label: E
children.text: 1 member shall represent the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission;

children.label: F
children.text: 5 members shall be selected by the Administrator from among State dam safety officials; and

children.label: G
children.text: 1 member shall be selected by the Administrator to represent the private sector.

children.heading: Nonvoting membership
children.label: 4 Nonvoting membership
children.text: The Administrator, in consultation with the Board, may invite a representative of the National Laboratories of the Department of Energy and may invite representatives from Federal or State agencies, representatives from nongovernmental organizations, or dam safety experts, as needed, to participate in meetings of the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall encourage the establishment and maintenance of effective programs, policies, and guidelines to enhance dam safety for the protection of human life and property throughout the United States.

children.heading: Coordination and information exchange among agencies
children.label: B Coordination and information exchange among agencies
children.text: In carrying out subparagraph (A), the Board shall encourage coordination and information exchange among Federal and State dam safety agencies that share common problems and responsibilities for dam safety, including planning, design, construction, operation, emergency action planning, inspections, maintenance, regulation or licensing, technical or financial assistance, research, and data management.

children.heading: Work groups
children.label: 6 Work groups
children.text: The Administrator may establish work groups under the Board to assist the Board in accomplishing its goals. The work groups shall consist of members of the Board and other individuals selected by the Administrator.

children.heading: Federal employees
children.label: A Federal employees
children.text: Each member of the Board who is an officer or employee of the United States shall serve without compensation in addition to compensation received for the services of the member as an officer or employee of the United States.

children.heading: Other members
children.label: B Other members
children.text: Each member of the Board who is not an officer or employee of the United States shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: Representatives of Federal agencies
children.label: A Representatives of Federal agencies
children.text: To the extent amounts are made available in advance in appropriations Acts, each member of the Board who represents a Federal agency shall be reimbursed of appropriations for travel expenses by his or her agency, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for an employee of an agency under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from the home or regular place of business of the member in the performance of services for the Board.

children.heading: Other individuals
children.label: B Other individuals
children.text: To the extent amounts are made available in advance in appropriations Acts, each member of the Board who represents a State agency, the member of the Board who represents the private sector, and each member of a work group created under paragraph (1) shall be reimbursed for travel expenses by FEMA, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for an employee of an agency under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from home or regular place of business of the member in performance of services for the Board.

children.heading: Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 9 Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to the Board.
